name: Arganda del Rey - Perales de Tajuña – circular por el Camino Uclés y Dehesa del Carrascal
region_intro: Mountain biking around Arganda del Rey offers diverse natural landscapes within the Sureste Regional Park, southeast of Madrid. The region is shaped by the Jarama River valley, featuring important wetlands and lagoons such as El Campillo Lagoon. Terrain includes Mediterranean forests, scrubland, pine forests, and holm oak stands, providing varied environments for mountain bike trails. The area offers a blend of tranquil natural spaces and more challenging terrain. Best mountain bike trails around Arganda Del Rey The most popular mountain bike trail is Arganda del Rey - Perales de Tajuña – circular por el Camino Uclés y Dehesa del Carrascal, a 29.3 miles (47.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route passes through the Dehesa del Carrascal, known for its natural beauty. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Campillo Lagoon – Rivas Ridges loop from Arganda del Rey, a moderate 27.6 miles (44.5 km) path. This trail offers scenic views around the El Campillo Lagoon and the Rivas Ridges. Local mountain bikers also love the Civil War Caves – Ascent to Las Cabras loop from Arganda del Rey, a 30.5 miles (49.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain including historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Mountain biking around Arganda Del Rey is defined by the Jarama River valley, the wetlands of El Campillo Lagoon, and varied Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Arganda Del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 1900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Arganda Del Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Arganda del Rey?
A: Arganda del Rey offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. You'll find paths through Mediterranean forests, scrubland, pine forests, and holm oak stands. The terrain is shaped by the Jarama River valley and includes wetlands and lagoons, providing varied environments for your rides. There are 15 easy routes, 69 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Arganda del Rey has 15 easy mountain bike trails that are great for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes allow you to enjoy the natural beauty of the Sureste Regional Park without requiring advanced technical skills.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many mountain bike trails in Arganda del Rey pass through the Sureste Regional Park, offering views of the Jarama River valley and its important wetlands. A prominent feature is the network of lagoons, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2572064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Campillo Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and Las Madres lagoons. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5123913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarama Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; or historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3300745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornicabra Station and Quarries&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Arganda del Rey?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Arganda del Rey are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962699744/arganda-del-rey-perales-de-tajuna-circular-por-el-camino-ucles-y-dehesa-del-carrascal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arganda del Rey - Perales de Tajuña loop&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through the Dehesa del Carrascal, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31924614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Lagoon – Rivas Ridges loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Arganda del Rey?
A: The mountain bike trails in Arganda del Rey are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. Many riders appreciate the varied terrain, the scenic views around the lagoons, and the well-maintained paths within the Sureste Regional Park.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike trail to explore the lagoons?
A: For a moderate ride exploring the lagoons, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38216326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Campillo – Puente Azul loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt;. This 42 km trail leads you through the natural beauty surrounding El Campillo Lagoon and typically takes about 3 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that combine nature with historical interest?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40375537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civil War Caves – Ascent to Las Cabras loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt; offers a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. This moderate 49 km route allows you to explore the landscape while encountering points of historical interest.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Arganda del Rey?
A: The best times for mountain biking in Arganda del Rey are generally spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be quite hot, while winters are typically mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Arganda del Rey dog-friendly?
A: The trails in Arganda del Rey are part of the Sureste Regional Park, which is a protected natural area. While many natural spaces in Spain allow dogs, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Specific regulations regarding dogs may vary, so check local signage.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Arganda del Rey?
A: There are nearly 100 mountain bike trails available around Arganda del Rey, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 14 difficult mountain bike routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10383543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civil War Caves – View of Ambite loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 95.7 km trail with significant elevation changes.
tips: From the Tajuña greenway you can see many cliffs and cliffs of gypsum and limestone excavated for thousands of years by the river. At the height of Tielmes, you can see some cavities that are machine gun nests, lookouts, trenches and bunkers from the civil war built by the Republican army to contain the advance of the national troops on the capital.

waypoints.name: View of the Caves Cliff in Tielmes
